findings.statement: CG005F closes the route weekly replacement revalidation with nine findings corrected, F-04 partial, F-08 failed, and 70/70 tests passing, but the tests are mostly mock/static and local PostgreSQL remains required for later behavioral proof.
evidence: research/CG005F_CORRECTION_REVALIDATION.json verdict=VALIDATED_WITH_CORRECTIONS
F-01, F-02, F-03, F-05, F-06, F-07, F-09, F-10, and F-11 are CORRECTED.
F-04 is PARTIAL and F-08 is FAILED.
tests ran=70 passed=70 failed=0; no real PostgreSQL execution.
No DB, no DDL, no apply, and no Supabase writes were authorized.
findings.next_required_action: Corregir exclusivamente F-08 y F-04 en un worktree no-apply antes de cualquier laboratorio PostgreSQL local o apply.

findings.statement: The implementation conceptually calls pg_advisory_xact_lock(bigint, bigint) by passing two hashtextextended results; PostgreSQL exposes pg_advisory_xact_lock(bigint) and pg_advisory_xact_lock(integer, integer), so the current two-bigint signature has no valid overload.
evidence: research/CG005F_CORRECTION_REVALIDATION.json validation_8_f08_lock classification=INVALID_SIGNATURE
acquire_week_assignment_lock uses pg_advisory_xact_lock(hashtextextended(%s, 0), hashtextextended(%s, 0)).
hashtextextended(text, bigint) returns bigint.
PostgreSQL function resolution has no pg_advisory_xact_lock(bigint, bigint) overload.
The failure occurs before writes and therefore fails closed; mocked tests do not validate catalog signature.
findings.next_required_action: Use one deterministic bigint advisory-lock key per week/contract, or a true two-int form, and validate it against local PostgreSQL only after explicit authorization.

findings.statement: Rollback restoration is present but incomplete because it commits without proving restored hash equality, weekly view consistency, resolved view consistency, missing/extra grains equal zero, and final equivalence before commit.
evidence: research/CG005F_CORRECTION_REVALIDATION.json validation_5_f04_rollback classification=PARTIAL
Rollback restores public.ruta_rutero from previous batch history, marks current assignment ROLLED_BACK, and reactivates the previous assignment.
It does not assert restored current_surface_hash equals the previous assignment stored hash.
It does not revalidate weekly/resolved views or resolved-vs-assigned grain sets before commit.
tests 54/55 assert source substrings rather than full rollback behavior.
findings.next_required_action: Run all rollback postchecks before commit and abort on any difference.
